name: German Egg Dumplings with Chervil
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Minced chervil or Italian flat-leaved parsley (minced)
1/2 cup Milk or water (or more as needed)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine all ingredients except butter, adding enough liquid so that batter is the consistency of a thick crepe batter.
2. With a wooden spoon or spatula, beat batter in a circular motion to incorporate as much air as possible into the dough (about 5 minutes). Allow dough to rest for 10 minutes.
3. Beat again another 5 minutes.
4. Bring lightly salted water (or stock) to a boil. Reduce heat so that water just simmers.
5. Using a large-holed colander, vegetable mill, spatzle cutter, or pastry bag, drop small bits of dough into the simmering liquid. If using a colander, press batter through holes; with a pastry bag, break off small bits with a knife or place a flat, large-holed cheese grater over the pot and press batter through openings with a spatula.
6. When cooked, spätzle will rise to the surface (about 5 minutes).
7. Remove with a slotted spoon, rinse under cold water, and drain well. To keep ahead of time, place spätzle on a damp towel and refrigerate, up to a day or two.
8. To reheat, place butter in a large baking pan and place in a moderate oven to melt the butter. Spread spätzle on the pan and toss to coat with butter. Heat, uncovered, in a 350 F. (175 C.) oven until golden brown and slightly crisp. Alternatively, sauté in butter.
9. Good served with veal or pork chops and sour cream gravy.
